title: Full benchmark suite and external scanner differential testing
description:
Build the full performance benchmark suite and extend differential testing to cover grammars with external scanners.

This builds on the earlier corpus testing bead, adding performance measurement and external scanner validation.

Deliverables:
- Benchmark suite: parse corpus of files at various sizes (1KB, 10KB, 100KB, 1MB) across JSON, Go, JavaScript, Python.
- Incremental parse benchmarks: single char edit, line insert, multi-line edit at various file sizes.
- Memory profiling: report allocations per parse, GC pause times via runtime.MemStats and GODEBUG=gctrace=1.
- Comparison benchmarks: same files through C tree-sitter and Go, report ratio with confidence intervals. Alternate runs (C, Go, C, Go) to average thermal effects.
- External scanner differential testing: parse files exercising external scanner tokens (template literals, indentation, heredocs) and compare with C tree-sitter.
- External scanner serialization roundtrip tests: serialize state, deserialize into fresh scanner, verify continued parsing matches.
- CI integration: benchmark-action/github-action-benchmark for regression detection (alert at 150% threshold).

Performance Targets:
| Operation | C (typical) | Go target |
|-----------|-------------|-----------|
| Parse 1KB JSON | ~50 us | < 200 us |
| Parse 10KB Go source | ~500 us | < 2 ms |
| Incremental reparse (1 char edit, 10KB) | ~5 us | < 50 us |
| Query match (simple, 10KB) | < 100 us | < 500 us |

Tests:
- Benchmark reproducibility (coefficient of variation < 10%)
- Differential testing passes for all grammars with external scanners

Reference: docs/testing-strategy.md §4-7, docs/design.md §8 Performance
